rulings.Case Title: BILL MOUNSEY et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY (SFM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication And Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 20, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY (SFM, EDWARD D. REISKIN, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS, TOM NOLAN, CHERYL BRINKMAN IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS VICE, MALCOLM HEINICKE, JERRY LEE, LEONA BRIDGES, JOEL RAMOS and CRISTINA RUBKE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication And Evidence. (part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) Granted. Resolution 12-110 was not illegally enacted by an administrative agency. A charter city has "broad[] authority to structure and organize [its] government." (Dibb v. County of San Diego (1994) 8 Cal.4th 1200, 1207.) A city charter can lawfully designate a city agency other than the city council or board of supervisors as "a legislative body of the¿municipality," and that agency can possess legislative authority as broad as that of the city council or board of supervisors. (City of Oakland v. Hogan (1940) 41 Cal.App.2d 333, 341-45.) The voters passed Proposition A, a charter amendment, which explicitly assigned the City's legislative power "over taxi-related function s and taxi-related fares, fees, budgets, and personnel," and over "motor vehicles for hire," to SFMTA. " Even though the SFMTA Board cannot enact ordinances¿the SFMTA Board was empowered by Proposition A¿to enact measures that override previous City ordinances concerning taxi service." (Yesson v. SFMTA (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 108,122.) Second, Resolution 12-110 was not enacted in violation of due process. Section 2.105 of the San Francisco Charter unambiguously pertains only to the Board of Supervisors. Further, Plaintiffs do not establish that the SFMTA was required to act by ordinance. Cities may adopt taxi regulations "by ordinance or resolution." (Cal. Veh. Code sec. 21100(b).) Third, Defendants have established that the Medallion Transfer Price is a charge "for the use of local government property," and is therefore not a tax per Cal. Constitution Article XIIIC, Section 1. In Cotta v. City and County of San Francisco (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 1550, 1561, it was held that "subject[ing] a street to the conduct or private business¿when authorized, is a special or extraordinary privilege¿[that] is an added easement or burden on the street, and is not comparable to the right to conduct lawful business on private property." Fourth, Plaintiffs have not established that the Medallion Transfer Program is contrary to promises made to those on the waitlist. It is undisputed that no specific promises were made that the medallion issuing scheme would never be altered. (UMF 12.) Further, Gov. Code sec. 9606 provides that "any statute may be repealed at any time, except when vested rights would be impaired." "[T]axicab drivers do not obtain any vested right in the grant of permission to operate taxicabs on the public roadways¿" (Cotta, 157 Cal.App.4th at 1560.) Further, "it is clear that neither the doctrine of equitable estoppel nor any other equitable principle may be invoked against a governmental body where it would operate to defeat the effective operation of a policy adopted to protect the public." (Kajima/Ray Wilson v. Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transp. Auth. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 305, 316.) It is undisputed that one of the reasons the SFMTA imposed the Medallion Transfer Program was to promote safety. (UMF 13.) (end of part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DUANE PAPIERNIAK VS. ARCHIVES MANAGEMENT INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 20, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT 1030 POLK ASSOCIATES LP's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled. Assuming Plaintiff's pleadings satisfy CCP § 474, in order to defeat "relation back" of an amendment joining "Doe" Defendants, the burden is on Defendant to prove-either on motion for summary judgment or at trial-that Plaintiff knew Defendant's identity and the facts giving rise to Defendant's liability. (Fuller v. Tucker (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 1163, 1173 [emphasis added].) The allegations contained in the Complaint are accepted as true when ruling on a Demurrer, and here the Complaint alleges that the true names of Defendants sued as Does were unknown to Plaintiff when the Complaint was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TONGA PARTNERS LP, et al VS. LOWENSTEIN SANDLER LLP, A NEW JERSEY LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Relief For Late Submitted Answers To Special Interrogatories And Responses To Req. For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 20, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS TONGA PARTNERS LP, CANNELL CAPITAL, LLC, J. CANNELL's Motion For Relief For Late Submitted Answers To Special Interrogatories And Responses To Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Pro Tem Judge has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY "SINTEZ" DIRECTLY AND VS. ALEXANDRE BAPA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 20, 2014, Line 14, Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. The court grants the OSC regarding preliminary injunction in its entirety. Plaintiff is entitled to injunctive relief pending the arbitration per CCP 1281.8. The Bayan declarations clearly show that he is the manager and defendant is engaging in improper self-help. Defendant's reference to the statement of information with the Secretary of State does not show that defendant is the manager. It merely shows that he is a member of Sintez USA. Defendant's partnership agreement argument fails because the operating agreement is a fully integrated instrument. Defendant's standing argument fails. Russian Sintez is not improperly transacting intrastate business b/c: (1) there is no proof that it is entering into "repeated and successive transactions" (Corp. 191(a)); (2) being a member of a domestic limited liability company is not "transacting intrastate business" (Corp. 191(b)(5)); and (3) maintaining a lawsuit is not "transacting intrastate business" (Corp. 191(c)(1)). Plaintiff is likely to suffer irreparable harm. The assets of Sintez USA are unique and injunctive relief is appropriate to insure that the plaintiff is not left with a hollow judgment. See Heckmann v. Ahmanson (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 119, 136. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The order should also list the relief requested in the TRO/OSC. =(302/EHG)
